definition: The Fed's revealed reaction function shows a ~200bp asymmetry: easing signaled at core PCE ~3.2% (~100bp above target) while tightening delayed until ~5.2% (~300bp above target), producing a de facto long-run inflation average of 2.3-3.0% and rendering the 2% target operationally non-binding.
evidence: Easing signaled at core PCE ~3.2% (2023), tightening delayed until ~5.2% (2022)
Political cost of recession exceeds political cost of moderately above-target inflation
1966-69 analogue: premature easing during inflation stall produced second wave 3/3 times
Based on observable Fed behavior, not estimated parameters
open_questions: Can the Fed's true tolerance be determined from a single cycle's revealed preferences?
Will the Fed framework review (2025-2026) formalize a higher effective target?
What are the cross-asset implications if the 2% target is operationally non-binding?
